texto: All of the stuff I can't afford to do on a TV budget, I just put into the comic book because you're really only limited in a comic by your artist's imagination.
explicacao: Citação de Marc Guggenheim sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “All of the stuff I can't afford to do on a TV budget, I just put into the comic book because you'…” articula reflexao, book, artist em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como artist e budget.

seo.titleSeo: "All of the stuff I can't afford to do on a TV budget…" — Marc Guggenheim | Metamensagem

frase_original: All of the stuff I can't afford to do on a TV budget, I just put into the comic book because you're really only limited in a comic by your artist's imagination.

texto: Fortunately, the DC Universe is full enough and replete enough with every kind of character that you could want, that it's not that hard to find the right character. Sometimes it's nothing more than an Easter egg, or a name drop, and sometimes it's someone like 'Deathstroke,' who is a huge part of the DC Universe.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Marc Guggenheim: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Fortunately, the DC Universe is full enough and replete enough with every kind of character that …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, character, universe pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e universe.

frase_original: Fortunately, the DC Universe is full enough and replete enough with every kind of character that you could want, that it's not that hard to find the right character. Sometimes it's nothing more than an Easter egg, or a name drop, and sometimes it's someone like 'Deathstroke,' who is a huge part of the DC Universe.
